key_insights.insight: Love and relationships are distinct; love is a feeling, while relationships require compatibility, work, and shared progress.
key_insights.quote: Love and relationships are two different things. Love is feeling, uh, relationships are, uh, compatibility and fit and work.

key_insights.insight: Relationships evolve, requiring partners to 'fall in love' repeatedly as individuals change over time.
key_insights.quote: I believe that Nibel and I had to fall in love six times, uh, uh, over, over the 28 years, because we both changed every single time.

key_insights.insight: Maintaining respect and friendship after a romantic separation is possible and valuable, preserving the positive impact of the relationship.
key_insights.quote: You can't kill that because you no longer want to sleep together. Do you understand this? You can't be that stupid to take all of those beautiful relationships and just say, okay, that's it. We're separated.

key_insights.insight: The speaker values the continued influence and impact of past relationships on their personal growth.
key_insights.quote: Her spirituality, her wisdom, her love made me who I am, okay?

key_insights.insight: Trust and understanding can remain even after romance fades.
key_insights.quote: I think we still carry each other's credit cards and we still manage our investments together and there is total trust and total understanding

actionable_advice.advice: Reassess and redefine the relationship periodically.
actionable_advice.how_to: Have open conversations about individual growth and evolving needs to ensure the relationship remains fulfilling.
Focus on shared history, mutual respect, and co-parenting if applicable.  Avoid cutting off all ties and instead nurture a friendship.

memorable_stories: The story of the speaker's 28-year relationship and amicable separation, maintaining friendship and shared financial arrangements.
The anecdote of 'falling in love six times' with the same person as both partners evolved.
contrarian_claims: Love is short-lived and requires renewal through repeated 'falling in love' experiences within the same relationship.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 45 - Why 80% Of Relationships Don't Work: Mo Gawdat
